sourceOfTruth: Runtime-ready means a built llama.cpp fork graph route selects the shipped Vulkan kernel and a numeric smoke test passes. SPIR-V compilation and pipeline symbols are not enough.

qjl.rerun: make -C packages/inference/verify vulkan-dispatch-smoke (Intel ARL / Mesa ANV): PASS 32 outputs, max diff 2.682e-7

Intel(R) Graphics (ARL) — Intel open-source Mesa ANV driver, Vulkan 1.4.318
fusedAttn.note: Single-q-pos fused attention graph route (n_heads=4 n_kv_heads=2 n_tokens=8) executed end-to-end on the built fork's libggml-vulkan and matched eliza_fused_attn_qjl_tbq3; the standalone fused shaders are 1920/1920 outputs PASS across 4 GQA/n_kv cases. fused_attn is an optimization on top of the five required kernels (AGENTS.md §3) and is intentionally NOT in requiredRuntimeCapabilityKeys / manifestKernelNames.
fusedAttn.rerun: make -C packages/inference/verify vulkan-dispatch-smoke (Intel ARL / Mesa ANV): PASS 512 outputs, max diff 4.470e-8

atCommitNote: libggml-vulkan built from fork 08032d57 (= ancestor of the committed eae44e75 pin; the ggml_attn_score_qjl / ggml_fused_attn_qjl_tbq builders + their Vulkan dispatch are unchanged between the two). The vulkan-dispatch-smoke harness (verify/vulkan_dispatch_smoke.cpp) was trimmed to the two fused attention graph ops the fork pin declares in ggml.h — GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_QJL and GGML_OP_FUSED_ATTN_QJL_TBQ. The turbo3/turbo4/turbo3_tcq/polar entries below record the prior full-patched-build run (GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_TBQ / GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_POLAR are extra builders the metal-kernels.mjs ggml.h patch adds during a build; those kernels stay runtime-ready on vulkan from that run and are also covered by `make vulkan-verify` against the JSON fixtures).
